seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Instructor enables reliable structured extraction from speech-to-text pipelines, ensuring LLM responses conform to Pydantic schemas for safe, predictable interactions. Guardrails are critical due to sub-second latency demands and high-stakes conversational risks like safety violations or incoherent dialogue. Vex provides runtime verification to catch Instructor's common failure modes before they disrupt real-time voice flows.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process real-time audio through a speech-to-text (STT) service like Deepgram or Whisper, feeding transcripts to an Instructor-patched LLM client for structured reasoning. Instructor wraps the LLM call to validate outputs against Pydantic models, retrying on failures like missing fields or hallucinated enums. The validated response triggers text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis, maintaining conversational state across turns.

Latency budgets (<500ms end-to-end) demand efficient verification, with Vex layered atop Instructor to enforce additional checks like response appropriateness and safety. Conversation coherence is tracked via session state, while nested extraction failures are mitigated through schema design and retry policies.

This pipeline handles interruptions, partial transcripts, and multi-turn context, requiring robust error recovery to prevent hangs or unsafe outputs.

risks.name: Structured output validation retries exhausted
risks.description: Instructor exhausts max_retries when LLM consistently fails schema validation, hanging the voice pipeline
risks.mitigation: Vex timeout enforcement + fallback unstructured response path

risks.name: Latency budget exceeded
risks.description: STT+LLM+verification exceeds 500ms, causing unnatural conversation pauses
risks.mitigation: Vex latency SLAs with streaming fallbacks

risks.name: Safety filter bypass
risks.description: Hallucinated responses evade content filters due to partial extraction

risks.name: Conversation incoherence
risks.description: Missing context fields break multi-turn dialogue flow
risks.mitigation: Vex coherence scoring against session state

implementation.title: Instructor Pydantic Model for Voice Response
implementation.description: Define structured response schema with conversation state and safety constraints
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Literal
from instructor import from_openai

class VoiceResponse(BaseModel):
    intent: Literal['greeting', 'question', 'command', 'farewell']
    response_text: str = Field(..., max_length=150)
    confidence: float = Field(..., ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    requires_escalation: bool = False
    conversation_state: dict[str, str] = {}

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

implementation.title: Basic Voice Pipeline with Instructor
implementation.description: STT → Instructor LLM → TTS pipeline with structured extraction
implementation.code:
async def voice_pipeline(transcript: str, session_id: str) -> VoiceResponse:
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",
        response_model=VoiceResponse,
        messages=[
            {"role": "system", "content": "You are a voice assistant. Always respond conversationally within 150 chars."},
            {"role": "user", "content": transcript}
        ],
        max_retries=3
    )
    return response

implementation.title: Real-time Latency Monitoring Wrapper
implementation.description: Track pipeline latency for voice SLAs
implementation.code:
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def latency_tracker(name: str, budget_ms: int):
    start = time.time()
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        duration = (time.time() - start) * 1000
        if duration > budget_ms:
            raise TimeoutError(f'{name} exceeded {budget_ms}ms: {duration:.0f}ms')

# Usage
with latency_tracker('voice_pipeline', 450):
    response = voice_pipeline(transcript, session_id)
